transcript.speaker: Jensen Huang
transcript.title: CEO
transcript.content: Good afternoon everyone and welcome to NVIDIA's third quarter earnings call. I'm pleased to report another record quarter with strong demand across our data center business. Our data center revenue reached new heights, driven by exceptional demand for our Hopper GPU computing platform. The world is experiencing two simultaneous transitions - accelerated computing and generative AI. Every company is racing to adopt these technologies, and NVIDIA is at the center of this transformation.

transcript.speaker: Colette Kress
transcript.title: CFO
transcript.content: Thank you, Jensen. Q3 was an outstanding quarter with record revenue of $35.1 billion, up 17% sequentially and up 94% year-on-year. Data Center revenue of $30.8 billion was a record, up 17% sequentially and up 112% from a year ago. Gaming revenue was $3.3 billion, up 14% sequentially and up 15% from a year ago. Professional Visualization revenue was $486 million, up 7% sequentially and up 17% from a year ago.

transcript.speaker: Jensen Huang
transcript.title: CEO
transcript.content: Looking ahead, we see incredible opportunities. The Blackwell platform is in full production and we expect to ship several billion dollars in Blackwell revenue in Q4. Demand for Blackwell is exceptional, and we are working to scale supply to meet this incredible interest from our customers. Every major cloud service provider and enterprise is looking to deploy our latest generation of AI infrastructure.
